title: OpenCL Irregular Access Bandwidth
question: How do gather, scatter, and random_both irregular access patterns compare across NVIDIA GPU, AMD GPU, and AMD CPU OpenCL devices when they share the same explicit permutation?
focus: Compare gather, scatter, and random_both on three OpenCL device classes
Use one explicit permutation per size and reuse it inside the timing interval
Keep permutation upload and validation outside the timed kernel interval
Observe whether random write remains more expensive than random read across vendors

considerations.en: gather, scatter, and random_both use explicit host-generated permutation indices over 4-byte elements. The permutation is generated once per size and copied once to the selected OpenCL device outside the timed kernel interval.

The same permutation is shared by gather and scatter so random-read and random-write penalties can be compared fairly. random_both uses the same permutation with a one-element rotation on the destination side.
The default size sweep is capped at 1 GiB because explicit permutation buffers are also 1 GiB at that size.

summary.en: Use compare.json to review the three-device comparison. The main watchpoint is whether random write patterns remain much slower than random read patterns across NVIDIA GPU, AMD GPU, and AMD CPU OpenCL implementations.
